channel.title: # 🇱🇰 This Week in Sri Lanka
channel.description: Generated by gpt-5 from 163 English News Articles published between 2026-04-28 and 2026-05-05.

item.title: Parliament received a Bill to overhaul Value Added Tax, cutting the threshold to Rs. 36 million and extending VAT to cross‑border digital services from July 2026.
item.description: The draft also raised VAT on financial services to 20.5 percent, increasing the sector’s tax burden relative to the 18 percent previously applied. Small and medium enterprises entered the net as the annual registration floor fell from Rs. 60 million to Rs. 36 million, with Rs. 9 million per taxable period replacing Rs. 15 million. Non‑resident platforms supplying digital services to users in Sri Lanka were required to register and file locally, while supplies to VAT‑registered businesses were excluded subject to documentation. Retailers faced a mandate to deploy secured point‑of‑sale systems capable of real‑time invoicing and data capture, and penalties for non‑compliance rose to a ceiling of Rs. 1 million with potential imprisonment for fraudulent refund claims. Export support was retained by zero‑rating services of garment buying offices when paid in foreign currency to persons outside Sri Lanka. The Inland Revenue Department reported Rs. 2,244 billion collected in 2025 against a target of Rs. 2,202 billion and set a 2026 target of Rs. 2,402 billion, with joint audits under an MoU (memorandum of understanding) with Sri Lanka Customs to strengthen enforcement.

item.title: Inflation accelerated to 5.4% year‑on‑year in April on the Colombo Consumer Price Index.
item.description: The rise from 2.2 percent in March reflected a 6.8 percent non‑food surge and a 2.8 percent food uptick, with transport costs driving a 2.43 percentage‑point contribution to the month‑on‑month increase. Core inflation also moved up to 2.8 percent from 2.5 percent. The Central Bank said the pass‑through of energy prices linked to Middle East disruptions pushed inflation toward its 5 percent medium‑term target band (±2 percentage points). The Bank projected inflation to remain around target if policy discipline held and supply shocks eased.

item.title: India’s High Commissioner urged Sri Lanka to fast‑track trade, energy and connectivity projects including a permanent land or tunnel link across the Palk Strait.
item.description: He called for an upgraded India–Sri Lanka Free Trade Agreement (FTA, a treaty that reduces tariffs and trade barriers) covering services and investment, noting that over 65 percent of Sri Lankan exports into India already used FTA preferences while only about 5 percent of Indian exports did. He highlighted that India was Sri Lanka’s largest trading partner and a leading investor, with Indian‑linked investments accounting for roughly half of 2025 FDI. The envoy pressed for a submarine power interconnection, refined‑product pipelines and digital public infrastructure interoperability to stabilise energy and payments. He said a bridge or tunnel over the 30‑kilometre Rameswaram–Talaimannar span would change regional logistics, contrasting it with today’s 300‑kilometre sea route between Colombo and Chennai.

item.title: Sri Lanka and Maldives signed seven MoUs during a State visit that marked 61 years of diplomatic relations.
item.description: The agreements covered counter‑terrorism, defence education, tourism cooperation, teacher training, youth and sports, health collaboration and archival cooperation. Both Governments confirmed reciprocal land allocation for new High Commissions in Colombo and Malé, signalling long‑term institutional ties. The leaders discussed fisheries processing, boat‑building and a possible Bank of Maldives presence in Sri Lanka to deepen financial links. A bilateral business forum was scheduled to explore investment in IT, real estate, and urban infrastructure alongside twin‑destination tourism and improved air and maritime connectivity.

item.title: Police arrested more than 120 foreign nationals in Rajagiriya over an alleged coordinated financial fraud operation.
item.description: The suspects from several Asian and African countries were detained at a rented apartment complex and devices including laptops and mobile phones were seized for forensic analysis. Investigators said several suspects fled during the initial raid and that further arrests were being pursued by Welikada Police.

item.title: The Central Bank issued a system‑wide circular to tighten bank reconciliations and Board oversight after control failures led to a multi‑billion‑rupee fraud.
item.description: The directive cited weak internal controls, delayed reconciliations and inadequate management monitoring as undermining reliable financial reporting and raised penalties and documentation standards. It pressed Boards and audit committees to ensure timely exception resolution and strengthen governance over suspense and transit accounts.

item.title: The President said a series of corruption cases would move through courts this month, with at least one verdict expected.
item.description: He told May Day rallies that investigations led by the CID (Criminal Investigation Department) and the Bribery Commission had advanced since last year and warned of custodial sentences in 2026. He added that fiscal consolidation had reduced the Budget deficit to its lowest since 1977 while export earnings topped $17 billion in 2025.

item.title: A committee submitted proposals to enable overseas voting for over 1.5 million Sri Lankans abroad.
item.description: The report covered voter registration for migrant workers, students and dual citizens, options for embassy, postal or secure online voting, and campaign rules under the expenditure law. The Ministry said Cabinet consideration would follow broader consultations, with longer‑term reforms also studying advance or mobile voting for essential workers domestically.

item.title: The Asian Development Bank sharply downgraded 2026 growth for developing Asia to 4.7% and raised inflation to 5.2% amid Middle East disruptions.
item.description: The update assumed average oil at $96 per barrel in 2026 versus around $69 pre‑conflict and warned growth could fall to 4.2 percent under a severe escalation scenario. It advised targeted, time‑bound fiscal support, limited price suppression and central bank measures to anchor expectations while cushioning liquidity.

item.title: ADB and Japan launched ACCEL to support SME liquidity now and energy security over the medium term.
item.description: The initiative will deploy policy‑based loans, credit lines and guarantees to keep firms afloat amid fuel price volatility while scaling investment in renewables and efficiency. Japan will channel support through ADB trust funds and coordinate with JICA (Japan International Cooperation Agency) and JBIC (Japan Bank for International Cooperation) to accelerate preparation.

item.title: Prima Ceylon raised wheat flour prices by Rs. 5 per kilogram citing higher transport costs.
item.description: The company said the increase applied to its “milk” brand, moving the retail price from Rs. 220 to Rs. 225 per kilogram. The adjustment added to household cost pressures that have risen with fuel and logistics volatility since February.

item.title: The Construction PMI’s Total Activity Index eased to 57.1 in March from 70.3 in February, still indicating expansion.
item.description: Respondents cited fuel and raw material constraints, supplier delays and rising costs linked to Middle East shipping disruptions, even as new orders and hiring expanded. CT Smith noted reconstruction after Cyclone Ditwah and a multi‑year public investment pipeline but flagged risks from higher input prices, labour shortages and financing tightness.

item.title: IFC agreed to finance a 100 MW utility‑scale solar plant with WindForce, Sri Lanka’s first at that size.
item.description: The facility is expected to generate around 220 GWh annually and create over 3,000 jobs, with IFC providing up to $18 million via a local‑currency loan backed by the IDA PSW (International Development Association Private Sector Window) Local Currency Facility. The project includes provisions for future battery storage and advisory support on grid resilience and cybersecurity.
